meta.setting: Company store counter in French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in debt, supplies, and company pressure

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Mining commerce, merchandise, courthouse-town material, and store texture.

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining communities, settlement, work, and supply pressure.

image_prompt.subject: a company store clerk behind a crowded counter, weighing beans near an open ledger while miners, a ranch woman, and a road hand wait with silent resentment
image_prompt.scene: dim store interior; shelves of tools, sacks, canned goods, boots, lamp smoke, wet coats by the door, no readable account book or labels
image_prompt.composition: square interior with ledger and brass scale in the foreground, clerk hands centered, customers as tense silhouettes beyond the counter
image_prompt.camera: counter-height close view like an in-engine inventory-and-dialogue scene
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the smallest object, the ledger, feel like the heaviest thing in the room.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine store interior
render_traits: slightly simplified clerk and customer silhouettes
modest texture resolution on paper, brass, wood, cloth, and sacks
hard ambient occlusion under counter objects and shelf rows
warm lamp light cut by cold doorway light
social-pressure composition rather than cozy shop nostalgia
style_modifiers.palette: lamp amber, ledger cream, brass dull gold, sack tan, counter brown, coal shadow, faded calico, and wet-coat gray.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable ledger entries, labels, posters, prices, signs, or brand text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The clerk, ledger, scale, goods, and customers are fully readable.
Every visible prop and garment belongs plausibly to an 1899 company store.
The image shows credit pressure rather than cozy commerce.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
